round4_phase1_complete.horizon_definition: K is number of forward rows in prices_round_4_day_*.csv for the same (day, product), sorted by timestamp, starting at the trade timestamp row (tape step index, not wall-clock).
round4_phase1_complete.aggressor_definition: buy_aggr if trade price >= ask1 at (day,timestamp,product); sell_aggr if price <= bid1; else unknown (excluded from participant tables).

bullets.one_sentence: Aggressor-conditioned forward mids are mostly near zero in tight VEV books except Mark 22 sell on tight VEV_5300 (negative K=20) and Mark 67 buy on extract (large positive K=5/20 but n≈60/day); no single (Mark, side, product) shows large stable same-direction edge across all three days with n≥30 per day in tight regime.

Residuals of fwd_20_sym after (buyer,seller,product,spread_bucket) cell means are numerically ~0 for all major pairs — the simple baseline already explains conditional mean markouts; no strong second-order residual cluster.

Dominant edges Mark 01→Mark 22 and hydro duopoly Mark 14↔Mark 38 match round4work initial summary; weighted two-hop traffic peaks through Mark 55 as bridge (graph artifact), useful for conditioning not standalone PnL.

Same-(day,timestamp) bursts (≥3 prints) show slightly higher mean VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT forward mid change at K=20 than a random single-trade control (mean ~0.32 vs ~0.08 on pooled n≈1470 vs 2673), while K=100 signs differ — suggestive short-horizon pressure only, needs stricter matched-time control before trading.

Pair×aggressor buckets show Mark 22 as seller on several VEVs with most negative pooled short fwd on tight names (e.g. VEV_5300), consistent with providing liquidity into basket flow; use as avoid/fade signal rather than inventory (not simulated here).

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: After multi-symbol burst timestamps, extract mid drifts up slightly more at K=20 than random single-trade controls (same tape).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: mean fwd_ex_20 burst ≈ 0.32 vs control ≈ 0.08 (mid units)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.n: burst prints with valid fwd: 1470; control sample: 2673
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Control is random singles not propensity-matched; effect is small in mid units.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT when book spread bucket is tight show strongly positive short fwd (K=5 and K=20).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: day1 K20 mean ≈ 2.03 on n=58; similar magnitude days 2–3
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.n: ~58–61 prints per day in tight extract
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Small n; must confirm not one-shot level jumps; still candidate for gated follow.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 in tight book: day 1 shows negative K=20 fwd mid (≈-0.55, n=38) but days 2–3 flip sign — not a stable cross-day directional edge without further conditioning.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Downrank as tradeable until a regime split explains day-1 vs later.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Structural Mark 01 → Mark 22 basket ladder (1339 prints) — use as regime flag for coordinated wing selling.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: graph dominance not a scalar markout
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Not automatically profitable; informs when not to lean short vol blindly.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Mark 14 ↔ Mark 38 hydro duopoly (728+714 prints) — symmetric flow; mean-reversion style microstructure possible but not evidenced in baseline residuals.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: residuals near zero under simple cell baseline
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Likely stable bot schedule; directional alpha not found in Phase 1 tables.

no_edge_or_negative_findings: (buyer,seller,product) cell-mean baseline leaves near-zero residuals for major Mark pairs — no obvious mis-modeled bot mean to exploit without richer features.
Most aggressor×product×tight-bucket cells have |mean fwd_20| ≪ 0.5 mid units or unstable sign across days.
Hour bucket stratification not yet exported separately (spread bucket already used); marginal gain expected given coarse timestamps.
